title: lotus cedrosensis
side_effects: Lotus cedrosensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, respiratory issues, and stomach problems. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Excessive use of the plant's extracts can lead to liver damage and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Improper preparation and dosage can also result in seizures, tremors, and even coma in severe cases.
